summary: The Agent Catalog page content (agent cards, AI Provider Connections cards, Test Run modal, Provider Config modal, quick stats) never renders in the browser for the assigned admin/admin123 test account — the global SubscriptionGate (GAP-P6-PAYWALL) replaces the entire routed /dashboard/agents content with a 'Subscribe to unlock Aether' paywall for any account without an active paid subscription, and admin/admin123 is confirmed Free-plan. This blocks direct browser verification of every card/button/modal on this screen (protocol §3.2 points 1-3).
reproduction: 4. Observe: sidebar correctly highlights 'Agents' as the active nav item, topbar renders normally, but the main content area shows the 'Subscribe to unlock Aether' paywall (data-testid=subscription-paywall) instead of the Agent Configuration grid / Provider Connections grid described in design/screens/agents.html.

5. Confirm via network capture: none of GET /agents/catalog, /agents/providers, /agents/config, /agents/stats fire on this load (only the shared sidebar widget's GET /agents fires) -> the AgentsPage component tree (and its data loader) never mounts while gated.
6. Repeated in a second, independent Playwright session (fresh browser context, fresh login) -> identical paywall, identical network pattern.
expected: Per the brief, the catalog UI (cards, statuses, config modal, provider connections) should be directly exercisable in the browser to verify visual conformance and every interactive element.
observed: The entire routed page content is replaced by a paywall; zero catalog DOM elements are ever mounted for this account. Root cause is NOT specific to the Agents screen -- it is the same global gate already filed as MV-pricing-001 (BLOCKER, pricing screen tester) with root cause AETHER_REQUIRE_PAID_SUBSCRIPTION=true blocking a Free-plan account from all actionable dashboard content, including the /pricing page's own advertised 'Free: 5 agent runs/month' claim. This finding documents the SAME gate's impact specifically on the Agents-catalog screen's testability, and is cross-referenced rather than duplicated.

summary: GET /agents/runs?limit=<negative integer> returns an unhandled HTTP 500 (plain-text 'Internal Server Error', not a structured JSON error) instead of an honest 4xx validation error.
reproduction: 1. Log in as admin/admin123, obtain a bearer token.
3. Observe HTTP 500, body 'Internal Server Error' (text/plain, content-length 21, no JSON, no detail field).
4. Compare: limit=0 -> 200 []; limit=abc -> 422 structured Pydantic error; limit=99999 -> 200 (silently clamped to real row count via min(limit,200), no crash).
5. Reproduced twice, identical response both times (different cf-ray ids: a1ca44913c2cda91-PDX and a1ca4492bc63b876-PDX).
expected: A negative limit should be rejected with a structured 422 validation error (as happens for a non-numeric limit), matching the endpoint's own honest-error contract used everywhere else on this screen's matrix.
observed: list_runs(limit: int = 50) passes min(limit, 200) straight into 'ORDER BY "createdAt" DESC LIMIT %s' (apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:1123-1125 + apps/api/app/repositories/agent_run.py:76-84) with no lower bound; min(-5,200)==-5, and 'LIMIT -5' is invalid to PostgreSQL, raising an uncaught exception that becomes a bare-text 500. Not reachable via the shipped frontend UI (which never sends a negative limit), so this is a low-exploitability edge case rather than a user-facing break -- rated MEDIUM, not HIGH/BLOCKER.
